title: In-project generator (slim manifest — Class B/C)
description: A <id>.gen.json under products/<id>/generators/. Class B = file emitter (fields + file templates). Class C = integration/service connector (+ a provisioning check before the write). NO requires graph, NO host bootstrap — these run inside an EXISTING project (invoked from the Assistant, not Forge). The slim-manifest engine path interpolates ${field} into file paths + template bodies and writes them.

class.description: B = file emitter; C = integration/service connector

pack.description: owning product, e.g. eds, app-builder, commerce-saas, commerce

detect.description: optional: a marker that gates the generator to a matching workspace, e.g. fstab.yaml for EDS

provisioning.description: Class C only: an Adobe service that must be enabled before the generated config is useful. Surfaced as a check + help link before the write.

check.description: how to verify (e.g. cli:aio console ... / a docs link)

fields.description: user inputs; ${id} is interpolated into file paths + template bodies

files.description: files to emit. `path` + `template` are source refs; the build inlines the template body as `content`. `merge:true` means merge into an existing file (e.g. mesh.json) rather than create.

path.description: target path, ${field} interpolated, relative to project root

template.description: template file under templates/<gen-id>/. For xmlMerge, this is the XML node FRAGMENT to insert.

merge.description: deep-merge JSON into an existing file instead of create (fail-safe: never overwrite)

xmlMerge.description: insert the XML fragment (content) under an existing config file's parent chain, idempotent by idAttr, insert-only. Takes precedence over merge. Only works when the node sits directly under a non-attributed parent (di.xml/events.xml/db_schema.xml); attributed-parent configs (crontab group id, routes router id) use a postNote instead.

parent.description: slash-separated tag chain the fragment is inserted under; first segment == rootTag

idAttr.description: attribute on the fragment's root node used for sibling de-dup

rootTag.description: root element when creating the file from scratch

rootAttrs.description: explicit root attrs; else the engine's known Magento map by filename (di/events/crontab/routes/webapi/acl). Needed for db_schema.xml (not in the map).
